sections.paragraph:
ἀφθορία, ας, ἡ (s. φθείρω) soundness, lit. incorruption (corresp. to ἄφθορος Diod S 4, 7, 3; Artem. 5, 95; Phalaris, Ep. 70; BGU 1106, 11; 1107, 7; PGM 5, 376; 7, 544; Esth 2:2) of pure doctrine Tit 2:7.—M-M. TW.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

corresp. corresp. = corresponding(ly)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 156
